text.title: nginx-configuration Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for nginx-configuration. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Set worker_processes to auto and worker_connections to 4096 or higher for production servers.
rule.rationale: auto matches CPU core count. Each worker handles worker_connections simultaneous connections; 4096 supports high-traffic workloads without tuning per server type.

rule.statement: Enable gzip for all text content types with comp_level 6 and min_length 256. Always include gzip_vary on.
rule.rationale: Level 6 gives the best compression-to-CPU ratio. min_length 256 avoids compressing tiny responses where overhead exceeds gain. gzip_vary ensures proxies cache both compressed and uncompressed variants.

rule.statement: Allow TLSv1.2 and TLSv1.3 only. Disable session tickets. Use ssl_session_cache shared. Let's Encrypt no longer supports OCSP (deprecated August 2025) — remove ssl_stapling directives.
rule.rationale: TLS 1.0 and 1.1 are deprecated by RFC 8996 and fail PCI DSS compliance. ssl_session_tickets off prevents reuse of session keys that compromise forward secrecy. OCSP stapling is now unsupported by Let's Encrypt.

rule.statement: Add all five required security headers to every HTTPS server block: HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, and Permissions-Policy.
rule.rationale: Each header mitigates a specific attack class: HSTS forces HTTPS, X-Frame-Options prevents clickjacking, X-Content-Type-Options prevents MIME sniffing, Referrer-Policy limits information leakage, Permissions-Policy disables unneeded browser APIs.

rule.statement: Always pass X-Forwarded-For, X-Forwarded-Proto, X-Real-IP, and Host headers when proxying. Set proxy_read_timeout and proxy_connect_timeout explicitly.
rule.rationale: Without these headers, the backend application cannot determine the client's real IP or whether the original request was HTTP or HTTPS — critical for security logging and redirect logic.

rule.statement: Use least_conn for API backends where request duration varies. Use ip_hash only when sticky sessions are required and session-level state cannot be externalized.
rule.rationale: Round-robin distributes requests evenly but sends long requests to already-busy servers. least_conn routes to the server with fewest active connections, producing more even load in heterogeneous workloads. ip_hash creates an uneven distribution when clients are behind NAT.

rule.statement: WebSocket proxying requires upgrading the connection. Set Upgrade and Connection headers explicitly; set proxy_read_timeout to at least 3600s for long-lived connections.
rule.rationale: HTTP/1.1 WebSocket upgrades use the Upgrade header. Without Connection: upgrade, Nginx drops the connection. Short proxy_read_timeout kills idle WebSocket connections.

rule.statement: Define rate limit zones in http{} block. Apply limit_req with burst and nodelay to sensitive endpoints (login, API keys, password reset).
rule.rationale: Zones defined once and referenced in multiple locations. nodelay serves burst requests immediately rather than queuing them, preventing legitimate users from seeing delays during momentary traffic spikes.

rule.statement: Run nginx -t before every reload or deploy. Never reload without passing -t — a broken config silently keeps the old config running but the next reload will fail.
rule.rationale: nginx -t validates syntax and checks include paths without restarting. On managed systems, an invalid config on reload can leave the server running stale configuration for hours until noticed.

rule.statement: Run SSL Labs test (ssllabs.com/ssltest) for every new domain. Aim for A+ grade. Resolve any warnings before going live.
rule.rationale: SSL Labs checks protocol versions, cipher strength, certificate chain, HSTS, and 40+ other criteria. A+ grade means all current best practices are met and no known vulnerabilities are present.
